Design System
🎨Design System
Relowa POC · v0.1
Home / ADR-0002

ADR-0002 — Supabase Realtime Standalone

StatusAccepted — POC Validated
Date2026-05-09

Context

Relowa's UI requires live updates for several core flows:

  • Auction countdowns and live bids
  • Tender list updates as new tenders publish
  • Audit-log live tail for admin dashboards
  • (Phase 2) Live carrier GPS positions for logistics tracking

We need a real-time push mechanism with these properties:

  • Postgres CDC integration — writes through any path (Drizzle, raw SQL, pg_cron) automatically reach subscribers.
  • Self-hostable in EU region — KVKK compliance is non-negotiable for Turkish waste operators.
  • React-friendly client SDK — our frontend runs Next.js + React.
  • Operationally acceptable for a solo lead — no multi-node Kafka cluster.

Decision

💡

We will run only the supabase/realtime Docker image, standalone, pointed at our own Postgres. No other Supabase component is used. Auth, REST, Storage, Studio — none of them. We hand-roll those (Drizzle for DB access, Hono for REST, S3 + presigned URLs for storage, Drizzle Studio / TablePlus / Adminer for inspection).

Frontend uses @supabase/realtime-js directly. We do NOT install @supabase/supabase-js — that would pull in the full Supabase ecosystem and defeat the purpose.

Consequences

▲ Positive
  • Same developer experience as Supabase Cloud realtime: channel('tenders').on('postgres_changes', ...).
  • Hits Postgres logical replication directly — no manual event publication layer needed.
  • Self-hosted in our VPC; KVKK-clean — no data passes through a US-controlled managed service.
  • Operational cost is one stateless Fargate task at ~$15/month. No multi-node cluster overhead.
▼ Negative
  • One more container to operate. (Mitigation: Fargate auto-restarts; the image is widely deployed and battle-tested.)
  • DB_ENC_KEY quirks — must be exactly 16 ASCII characters for AES-128-ECB. Documented in docs/memory/learned/realtime-aes-key-length.md.
  • Tight coupling to Supabase's image release cadence. (Mitigation: pinned to v2.30.34; we evaluate changelog before bumping.)

Alternatives Considered

Alternative Verdict Reason
AWS AppSync subscriptions Rejected Managed and KVKK-friendly, but requires us to publish events explicitly — no CDC. More operational work, more GraphQL lock-in.
Pusher / Ably Rejected Best DX of any managed option, but third-party data residency adds KVKK paperwork and per-connection cost grows quickly at scale.
Postgres LISTEN/NOTIFY Rejected Zero additional infrastructure, but 8KB payload limit per notification and no built-in multi-server scaling. Acceptable for POC, insufficient for Phase 1.

Validation

✅

POC seeded the publication with tenders, bids, audit_events. Logical replication slot is active. Container is healthy and listening on port 4000. The Realtime health endpoint returns 200: curl -I http://localhost:4000/api/tenants/realtime-dev/health. End-to-end client subscription test in apps/web/ is in progress.

See Also
